Name: End Of The Day
Notes: Cover art on Yeat Archive for "End Of The Day."  The artwork is the Takashi Murakami for BLM Black Flowers Square Print (Signed, Edition of 300). Renowned Japanese artist Takashi Murakami provided support for the Black Lives Matter movement with the release of the Takashi Murakami for BLM Flowers Square Print. The print features Takashi’s iconic flower motif that is geometrically outlined over the print. The print is completely black, with a white border and a white outline on the flowers. Takashi said all proceeds from the prints were donated to U.S. organizations fighting for social justice and racial equality. The Takashi Murakami for BLM Flowers Square Print was limited to 300 prints, and was released in July 2020 for a retail price of $800.

Name:
up
(Tf up)
Notes: Cover art on Yeat Archive for "up." The artwork is the Takashi Murakami for BLM Black Flowers & Skulls Round Print (Signed, Edition of 300). Renowned Japanese artist Takashi Murakami provided support for the Black Lives Matter movement with the release of the Takashi Murakami for BLM Flowers Square Print. The print features Takashi’s iconic flower motif that is geometrically outlined over the print. The print is completely black, with a white border and a white outline on the flowers. Takashi said all proceeds from the prints were donated to U.S. organizations fighting for social justice and racial equality. The Takashi Murakami for BLM Flowers Square Print was limited to 300 prints, and was released in July 2020 for a retail price of $800.

Name:
ADL
(A Dangerous Lyfe, A Dangerous Love)
Notes:
OG Filename: 20260206_YEAT_ADL_CIAN_MOORE_SHOT_1_094
Alternate Filename: ADL DSP
Official cover art for ADL. The cover pays homage to a scene from 1993 crime/thriller drama A Bronx Tale, fitting with the mafia theme Yeat used during the promotion of the album. The cover combines two different scenarios, Lyfe and Love, where a shirtless Yeat witnesses himself getting married to French-Moroccan model Camelia Bourbon, and he also witnesses his own funeral. Shot at Christ Episcopal Church, 408 S Broadway, Redondo Beach, CA 90277 on February 6, 2026, modified on February 13, 2026. Announced by Yeat via socials on March 3, 2026.
